get.summary: Normal $ref path: Envelope appears in components and is $ref'd here.
get.description: Normal $ref path: Envelope appears in components and is $ref'd here.

get.summary: Inline response: the response schema is inlined, but Envelope is still in components (because GetEnvelope above $refs it), so the inline body $refs Envelope rather than expanding it.
get.description:
Inline response: the response schema is inlined, but Envelope is still in components
 (because GetEnvelope above $refs it), so the inline body $refs Envelope rather than
 expanding it.

schema.description:
A shared "envelope" type referenced from two RPCs: one that inlines its response, one that
 emits a $ref.  The non-inline reference keeps Envelope in components/schemas, and the inline
 reference $refs into that same component.

get.summary: Inline response of a type that is reached only through this inline path.
get.description:
Inline response of a type that is reached *only* through this inline path.  PrivateNote
 and its NoteAttachment field are therefore absent from components — both expand inline
 at this single site.

schema.description:
A type reached only through an inlined RPC response.  The transitivity rule omits it from
 components/schemas; its schema appears once, inline, at the response site.

attachment.description:
Reached only via PrivateNote, which is itself inline-only — so this transitively gets
 expanded inline too.

post.summary: Inline request: the body's referenced message (Envelope, via body: "envelope") is inlined at the requestBody site.
post.description:
Inline request: the body's referenced message (Envelope, via body: "envelope") is
 inlined at the requestBody site.  Envelope is also in components from the GetEnvelope
 RPC above, so its nested Payload $ref is preserved (Payload is in components too).

schema.description:
A shared "envelope" type referenced from two RPCs: one that inlines its response, one that
 emits a $ref.  The non-inline reference keeps Envelope in components/schemas, and the inline
 reference $refs into that same component.

Envelope.description:
A shared "envelope" type referenced from two RPCs: one that inlines its response, one that
 emits a $ref.  The non-inline reference keeps Envelope in components/schemas, and the inline
 reference $refs into that same component.

Payload.description:
A shared inner message embedded within Envelope.  Because it's reached from a non-inlined
 path (Envelope is in components), Payload is also in components.
